changes_summary: Revised plan to address all 4 flags: (1) Split computation into group detection (TimelineEditor) and pixel positioning (TimelineCanvas) so resizeOverrides and drag state are available where needed. (2) Fixed box geometry to use ACTION_VERTICAL_MARGIN offsets matching clip insets. (3) Added hover-label mechanism using a thin pointer-events-auto strip in the top margin with native tooltip. (4) Changed adjacency tolerance from 0.001s to SNAP_THRESHOLD_PX/pixelsPerSecond, matching existing snap behavior.
questions: Should the hover strip be visible at all times (as a thin colored bar above the clip group) or only appear on hover? Current plan: invisible until hover.
If a clip belongs to multiple shots, should it participate in bounding boxes for all of them (potentially overlapping boxes)?

success_criteria.criterion: Colored bounding boxes render around groups of 2+ adjacent same-track clips that belong to the same shot in image-array order
Bounding boxes do not interfere with clip selection, dragging, resizing, or context menus
Each shot gets a deterministic color derived from its ID that is stable across re-renders
Multiple shots can have visible bounding boxes simultaneously with different colors
Bounding boxes only appear for clips on the same track (not cross-track)
Bounding boxes only appear for clips within snap-threshold adjacency (SNAP_THRESHOLD_PX converted to time)
Bounding boxes only appear when clips are in the same order as the shot's images array
Bounding boxes disappear during active drag or resize operations
Box geometry matches clip insets (ACTION_VERTICAL_MARGIN) rather than spanning full row height
Shot name appears as tooltip when hovering over the bounding box area
All existing timeline tests continue to pass
New unit tests cover: adjacency, ordering, cross-track, single-clip, mixed shots, deterministic color, missing generationId
Group detection is memoized and only recomputes when rows, meta, registry, or shots change
Bounding box visual is subtle (low-opacity background, semi-transparent border) and does not obscure clip content
Color palette has at least 6 visually distinct colors that work on the dark timeline background
Hover strip does not interfere with clip drag initiation in the top margin area

assumptions: Adjacency tolerance matches existing SNAP_THRESHOLD_PX (8px) converted to time at current zoom level — clips that are close enough to snap together are close enough to group
A qualifying group requires minimum 2 clips — a single clip from a shot does not get a bounding box
Shot image order is determined by array index in shot.images[], not by position or timeline_frame fields
Clips without a generationId in their asset registry entry are silently excluded from grouping
The hover tooltip uses native browser title attribute rather than a custom tooltip component, keeping complexity minimal
During any active drag (dragSessionRef.current !== null) or resize (resizeOverrides non-empty), all bounding boxes are hidden rather than attempting real-time updates
The MAX_GAP_SECONDS upper bound (0.5s) in the grouping hook is intentionally generous — the precise pixel-aware check in TimelineCanvas does the real filtering
